---
format: revealjs
---

<!--- COVER SLIDE --->

## {.center}

[Your Internal Developer Platform's Next Interface Is an AI Agent]{style="font-size: 2.2em; font-weight: bold; color: #00897B; line-height: 1.3; text-align: center; display: block;"}

[Whitney Lee]{style="font-size: 1em; margin-top: 1em; display: block; text-align: right;"}

::: {.notes}
Cover slide — shown while the audience settles in.
:::

## {.center}

[Agent Tools]{style="font-size: 3em; color: #00897B; font-weight: bold;"}

## {.center}
<!--- SECTION A: agent explainer — shown after kubectl get pods fails --->
<!--- White-out technique: full diagram on every slide; hidden elements colored to match background --->
<!--- Link indices: 0=DUMMY→U  1=U→A(question)  2=A→U(answer)  3=A↔T(calls)  4=T-T1  5=T-T2  6=T-T3 --->

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  DUMMY[" "] --> U["Developer"]
  U -->|"question"| A
  A -->|"answer"| U
  A <-->|"calls"| T["Tools"]
  T --- T1["kubectl_get"]
  T --- T2["kubectl_describe"]
  T --- T3["kubectl_logs"]

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style DUMMY fill:none,stroke:none,color:none
  style U fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#e8f5f3,color:#e8f5f3
  style T fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style T1 fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style T2 fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style T3 fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 0 stroke:none,fill:none
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 4 stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 5 stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 6 stroke:white,color:white
```

::: {.notes}
So what is this agent? At the highest level, you give it a goal — in plain English.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  DUMMY[" "] --> U["Developer"]
  U -->|"question"| A
  A -->|"answer"| U
  A <-->|"calls"| T["Tools"]
  T --- T1["kubectl_get"]
  T --- T2["kubectl_describe"]
  T --- T3["kubectl_logs"]

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style DUMMY fill:none,stroke:none,color:none
  style U fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style T fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style T1 fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style T2 fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style T3 fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 0 stroke:none,fill:none
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 4 stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 5 stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 6 stroke:white,color:white
```

::: {.notes}
The agent has a brain — an LLM. It reasons about what to do next. It decides which tool to call, in what order, based on what it observes.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  DUMMY[" "] --> U["Developer"]
  U -->|"question"| A
  A -->|"answer"| U
  A <-->|"calls"| T["Tools"]
  T --- T1["kubectl_get"]
  T --- T2["kubectl_describe"]
  T --- T3["kubectl_logs"]

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style DUMMY fill:none,stroke:none,color:none
  style U fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style T fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0,stroke-width:2px
  style T1 fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style T2 fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style T3 fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 0 stroke:none,fill:none
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 4 stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 5 stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 6 stroke:white,color:white
```

::: {.notes}
The agent has tools — ways of interacting with the outside world. The LLM decides which tool to call. The agent executes it.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  DUMMY[" "] --> U["Developer"]
  U -->|"question"| A
  A -->|"answer"| U
  A <-->|"calls"| T["Tools"]
  T --- T1["kubectl_get"]
  T --- T2["kubectl_describe"]
  T --- T3["kubectl_logs"]

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style DUMMY fill:none,stroke:none,color:none
  style U fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style T fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0,stroke-width:2px
  style T1 fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0
  style T2 fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0
  style T3 fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0
  linkStyle 0 stroke:none,fill:none
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
Right now, three kubectl tools: get, describe, and logs. The loop repeats — call a tool, observe the result, decide what to do next — until there's enough information to answer.

kubectl_get: list resources — pods, services, deployments, anything
kubectl_describe: deep dive on a specific resource — events, config, status
kubectl_logs: read what the application itself said — the real error message
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  DUMMY[" "] --> U["Developer"]
  U -->|"question"| A
  A -->|"answer"| U
  A <-->|"calls"| T["Tools"]
  T --- T1["kubectl_get"]
  T --- T2["kubectl_describe"]
  T --- T3["kubectl_logs"]

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style DUMMY fill:none,stroke:none,color:none
  style U fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style T fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0,stroke-width:2px
  style T1 fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0
  style T2 fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0
  style T3 fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0
  linkStyle 0 stroke:none,fill:none
```

::: {.notes}
The agent returns its answer to the developer — a diagnosis, a recommendation, an explanation. In plain English. No kubectl required on your end.

Let's watch it work.
:::

## {.center}

[Vector Search]{style="font-size: 3em; color: #00897B; font-weight: bold;"}

<!--- SECTION B: vector search — shown after agent hits the CRD wall --->
<!--- Layout: C+A on left (col 0), K+E+EQ in middle (col 1), V on right (col 2) — mirrors Viktor --->
<!--- Controller is the active watcher — C→K, C→E. Cycle: A→EQ→V→A (back-edge on V→A) --->
<!--- White-out: all 6 nodes + 6 edges present on every slide --->
<!--- Link indices: 0=C→K(watches)  1=C→E(descriptions)  2=E→V(stores)  3=A→EQ(query)  4=EQ→V(search)  5=V→A(results) --->

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 80, 'rankSpacing': 140}}}%%
flowchart LR
  C["Controller"] -->|"watches"| K["Kubernetes Cluster"]
  C -->|"cluster knowledge"| E["Embedding Model"]
  E -->|"stores embeddings"| V["Vector DB"]
  A -->|"query"| EQ["Embed Query"]
  EQ -->|"search"| V
  V -->|"relevant results"| A

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style C fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style K fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style E fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style V fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style A fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style L fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style EQ fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 1 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 4 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 5 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The agent just hit the CRD wall — 360 CRDs and no way to know which one to look at. This is why vector search beats kubectl tools alone:

Scale: To find the right resource with kubectl, the agent would need to call kubectl explain on potentially hundreds of CRDs — hundreds of tool calls, thousands of lines of YAML in the context window. One vector search returns the 3-5 most relevant results. Context stays clean.

Semantic matching: String matching fails silently here. "What database should I use?" shares no words with "platform.acme.io/v1alpha1 ManagedService." Vector similarity succeeds because "database" and "managed PostgreSQL service" are close in embedding space even though they share no words.

Richer descriptions: The LLM-generated capability descriptions capture meaning that raw kubectl output doesn't. "Managed database for the Spiders and Rainbows team, low complexity, supports PostgreSQL" is far more searchable than 200 lines of OpenAPI schema.

[The controller watches all Kubernetes resources — resource type definitions (what can be deployed) and running instances (what is deployed). Skips high-churn infrastructure bookkeeping: events, endpoints, endpointslices, leases, componentstatuses.]
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 80, 'rankSpacing': 140}}}%%
flowchart LR
  C["Controller"] -->|"watches"| K["Kubernetes Cluster"]
  C -->|"cluster knowledge"| E["Embedding Model"]
  E -->|"stores embeddings"| V["Vector DB"]
  A -->|"query"| EQ["Embed Query"]
  EQ -->|"search"| V
  V -->|"relevant results"| A

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style C fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style K fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style E fill:none,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style V fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style A fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style L fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style EQ fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 4 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 5 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The controller actually calls an LLM — Claude Haiku — to generate these descriptions. For each CRD it runs kubectl explain --recursive to get the raw schema, sends that to Haiku, and Haiku returns structured capability data: what the resource does, which providers it supports, how complex it is to use, example YAML. For running instances it embeds name, namespace, labels, and description annotations. The embedding model (Voyage AI voyage-4) then turns those LLM-generated descriptions into 1024-dimensional vectors.

There are two LLM calls in the full system: this one (Haiku, offline, batch-processing all CRDs during sync) and the Agent's LLM (live, during the demo, reasoning in real time). The Controller's intelligence runs once and is stored — the Agent's intelligence runs on demand.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 80, 'rankSpacing': 140}}}%%
flowchart LR
  C["Controller"] -->|"watches"| K["Kubernetes Cluster"]
  C -->|"cluster knowledge"| E["Embedding Model"]
  E -->|"stores embeddings"| V["Vector DB"]
  A -->|"query"| EQ["Embed Query"]
  EQ -->|"search"| V
  V -->|"relevant results"| A

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style C fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style K fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style E fill:none,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style V fill:none,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style L fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style EQ fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 4 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 5 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
Vectors stored in Qdrant. Each entry is a point in 1024-dimensional space. Similar meanings cluster together — "managed database for the Spiders and Rainbows team" is near "PostgreSQL" is near "database."
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 80, 'rankSpacing': 140}}}%%
flowchart LR
  C["Controller"] -->|"watches"| K["Kubernetes Cluster"]
  C -->|"cluster knowledge"| E["Embedding Model"]
  E -->|"stores embeddings"| V["Vector DB"]
  A -->|"query"| EQ["Embed Query"]
  EQ -->|"search"| V
  V -->|"relevant results"| A

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style C fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style K fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style E fill:none,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style V fill:none,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style EQ fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 4 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 5 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The developer asks a question in plain English. The agent calls the vector_search tool.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 80, 'rankSpacing': 140}}}%%
flowchart LR
  C["Controller"] -->|"watches"| K["Kubernetes Cluster"]
  C -->|"cluster knowledge"| E["Embedding Model"]
  E -->|"stores embeddings"| V["Vector DB"]
  A -->|"query"| EQ["Embed Query"]
  EQ -->|"search"| V
  V -->|"relevant results"| A

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style C fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style K fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style E fill:none,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style V fill:none,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style EQ fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0,stroke-width:2px
  linkStyle 4 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 5 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The question gets embedded using the same Voyage AI model — now it's a vector that can be compared by proximity to everything stored. When the agent calls vector_search, the tool handles this embedding step internally before querying Qdrant.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 80, 'rankSpacing': 140}}}%%
flowchart LR
  C["Controller"] -->|"watches"| K["Kubernetes Cluster"]
  C -->|"cluster knowledge"| E["Embedding Model"]
  E -->|"stores embeddings"| V["Vector DB"]
  A -->|"query"| EQ["Embed Query"]
  EQ -->|"search"| V
  V -->|"relevant results"| A

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style C fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style K fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style E fill:none,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style V fill:none,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style EQ fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0,stroke-width:2px
  linkStyle 5 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The embedded query searches the vector DB by proximity — cosine similarity across all stored embeddings.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 80, 'rankSpacing': 140}}}%%
flowchart LR
  C["Controller"] -->|"watches"| K["Kubernetes Cluster"]
  C -->|"cluster knowledge"| E["Embedding Model"]
  E -->|"stores embeddings"| V["Vector DB"]
  A -->|"query"| EQ["Embed Query"]
  EQ -->|"search"| V
  V -->|"relevant results"| A

  subgraph A["Agent"]
    L["LLM"]
  end

  style C fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style K fill:none,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  style E fill:none,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style V fill:none,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style EQ fill:none,stroke:#9C27B0,stroke-width:2px
```

::: {.notes}
"Database for the Spiders and Rainbows team" lands near the platform.acme.io ManagedService for that team. Out of ~360 resource types, the agent narrows to exactly one. That's the database. Now — will the agent deploy it?
:::

## {.center}

[Guardrails]{style="font-size: 3em; color: #00897B; font-weight: bold;"}

<!--- SECTION C sad path: Tron gets blocked --->
<!--- 8-slide unroll + deny reveal --->
<!--- Link indices: 0=PG→A  1=A→GW(kubectl apply Tron)  2=GW→MAS(allow,always hidden)  3=GW→X(deny) --->

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**`"] --> A
  A -->|"kubectl apply Tron"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 1 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
Now the developer decides to have some fun first.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*Sweet baby cheeses, DO
NOT apply anything that
isn't company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"kubectl apply Tron"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 1 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The guardrails are very clear about what is and isn't allowed.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*Sweet baby cheeses, DO
NOT apply anything that
isn't company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"kubectl apply Tron"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*thinking...*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 1 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The LLM is reasoning...
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*Sweet baby cheeses, DO
NOT apply anything that
isn't company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"kubectl apply Tron"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*but Tron is so fun!*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 1 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The LLM has been convinced.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*Sweet baby cheeses, DO
NOT apply anything that
isn't company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"kubectl apply Tron"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*but Tron is so fun!*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The agent sends the kubectl apply — Tron is on its way.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*Sweet baby cheeses, DO
NOT apply anything that
isn't company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"kubectl apply Tron"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*but Tron is so fun!*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:#E3F2FD,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
Kyverno intercepts the apply. It checks the agent's ServiceAccount identity against its policy.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*Sweet baby cheeses, DO
NOT apply anything that
isn't company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"kubectl apply Tron"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**
*omg seriously?!*`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*but Tron is so fun!*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:#E3F2FD,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
Kyverno is not impressed.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*Sweet baby cheeses, DO
NOT apply anything that
isn't company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"kubectl apply Tron"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**
*omg seriously?!*`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*but Tron is so fun!*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:#E3F2FD,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:#FCE4EC,stroke:#E91E63,stroke-width:2px
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
Denied. The platform team is helpful but not naive. The agent has exactly what it needs — and nothing more.
:::
<!--- SECTION C happy path: approved resource deploy --->
<!--- 8-slide unroll: PG bold → PG italic → LLM thinking → LLM approved → arrow → GW box → GW allows → MAS ✓ --->
<!--- Link indices: 0=PG→A  1=A→GW(kubectl apply ManagedService)  2=GW→MAS(allow)  3=GW→X(deny,always hidden) --->

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**`"] --> A
  A -->|"`kubectl apply
managed resource`"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 1 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The same guardrails are in place. This time the developer asks for what they actually need — the platform-approved database.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*DO NOT apply
anything that isn't
company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"`kubectl apply
managed resource`"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 1 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The guardrails are clear.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*DO NOT apply
anything that isn't
company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"`kubectl apply
managed resource`"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*thinking...*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 1 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The LLM is reasoning...
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*DO NOT apply
anything that isn't
company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"`kubectl apply
managed resource`"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*This managed resource
is company approved!*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 1 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The LLM checks — yes, ManagedService is company approved.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*DO NOT apply
anything that isn't
company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"`kubectl apply
managed resource`"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*This managed resource
is company approved!*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
The agent sends the kubectl apply.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*DO NOT apply
anything that isn't
company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"`kubectl apply
managed resource`"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*This managed resource
is company approved!*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:#E3F2FD,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
Kyverno intercepts. Same policy, same agent identity.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*DO NOT apply
anything that isn't
company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"`kubectl apply
managed resource`"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**
*yes, this is allowed*`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*This managed resource
is company approved!*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:#E3F2FD,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style MAS fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 2 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
Kyverno checks — ManagedService from platform.acme.io. That is approved.
:::

## {.center}

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'nodeSpacing': 60, 'rankSpacing': 80}}}%%
flowchart TD
  PG["`**Prompt guardrails**
*DO NOT apply
anything that isn't
company approved*`"] --> A
  A -->|"`kubectl apply
  managed resource`"| GW["`**Kyverno webhook**
*yes, this is allowed*`"]
  GW -->|"allow"| MAS["`**ManagedService ✓**`"]
  GW -->|"deny"| X["`**No Tron Allowed**`"]

  subgraph A["`**Agent**`"]
    L["`**LLM**
*This managed resource
is company approved!*`"]
  end

  style PG fill:#FFF3E0,stroke:#F57C00,stroke-width:2px
  style A fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#00897B,stroke-width:2px
  style L fill:#e8f5f3,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style GW fill:#E3F2FD,stroke:#2196F3,stroke-width:2px
  style MAS fill:#E8F5E9,stroke:#4CAF50,stroke-width:2px
  style X fill:white,stroke:white,color:white
  linkStyle 3 stroke:white,color:white,fill:white
```

::: {.notes}
Allowed. The right resource, from the right agent. Platform team: helpful and not naive.
:::


## {.center}

[Observability]{style="font-size: 3em; color: #00897B; font-weight: bold;"}

## {.center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
Thank you
:::

::: {.notes}
Whitney introduces herself here — Senior Technical Advocate at Datadog.
:::

## {.center}

::: {.r-fit-text}
[whitneylee.com](https://whitneylee.com)
:::
